etymology.description: 犬 (dog) + 口 (mouth, corrupted to 甘 and later 日) + ⺼ (meat) -> a dog eating meat -> satisfied
etymology.interpretation: The character 猒 originally depicted a dog 犬 (quǎn, 'dog') eating from a mouth 口 (kǒu, 'mouth') filled with meat ⺼ (ròu, 'meat'), illustrating a state of being 'satisfied' after consuming a meal. Over time, the component 口 transformed into 甘 (gān, 'sweet') and then 日 (rì, 'sun'), keeping the basic imagery of fulfillment or satisfaction intact. This concept also extends to a feeling of weariness that often follows complete satisfaction.
